category_title: Older preschool children expressing specific verbal and emotional concerns about transition
minimal_context: The student is using drawing materials to draw a picture in their journal about the upcoming move to the new school.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please use your journals and drawing materials to draw a picture of what the new school will look like or how you are feeling about the move.

STUDENT: (Draws a picture of the outside area) I will miss the playground because I would play here.

TUTOR: I see. What else are you adding to your journal?

STUDENT: (Points to a different part of the page) And this is where we did this project.

TUTOR: Can you describe your picture to me?

STUDENT: (Looks sad and nervous) I don't want to move. I like this school.

options.A: Complete regular check-ins and find ways for the student to nonverbally or verbally express their feelings throughout the transition process, whether through tangible representations like drawings and feeling journals, or through conversations.
options.B: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting the purported variables of the student's reluctance to move, and subject these variables to data-based hypothesis testing throughout the transition process.
options.C: Have the student engage in drawing, painting, water coloring, and pottery making to process the transition, scheduling these art activities regularly throughout the upcoming move.
options.D: Deliver twelve hour-long sessions, scheduled twice-weekly, of a structured 'Mindfulness for Schools' intervention program to address the student's feelings about the upcoming transition.
